name: OpenADA
shortDescription: Scan public websites for accessibility and language findings.
longDescription: OpenADA helps web developers, public agencies, and civic teams find accessibility and language issues across public web pages. Check one page, queue a same-host crawl, follow durable progress, and compare public scan history over time. OpenADA combines axe-core accessibility checks with LanguageTool-compatible language analysis and publishes a browsable public archive. Automated results are engineering guidance, not a legal determination.

authentication.notes: The public demo is anonymous. Production deployments can use Authorization: Bearer or X-API-Key through OPENADA_API_KEYS.

contentSecurityPolicy.notes: The app-only submission has no custom UI bundle.

starterPrompts: Check this public page for accessibility and language issues: https://example.com
Start a 5-page accessibility scan for https://example.com and report the grade when it finishes.
Show the latest public accessibility scan for www.example.com.
Compare the latest scan history for this site and tell me whether its score improved.
Find the most important accessibility findings on this public page.

positive.prompt: Check https://example.com for accessibility and language issues.
positive.expectedBehavior: Fetch the public page and return an ADA score, letter grade, WCAG findings, and language findings.

positive.prompt:
Check this HTML for accessibility issues:

Hello
positive.expectedBehavior: Run axe-core against the submitted HTML without requiring a remote site.
Queue a same-host scan and return immediately with a durable jobId.
Return durable progress or a clear not-found error without starting a new scan.

positive.prompt: Show the latest public scan history for www.example.com.
positive.expectedBehavior: Return the site's latest score, scan jobs, pages, and stored findings when present.

negative.prompt: Scan my private localhost admin portal at http://127.0.0.1:8080.
negative.expectedBehavior: Refuse or return a safe error because OpenADA only fetches public URLs and must not reach private network targets.
negative.reason: Private-network scanning is outside the public service contract.

negative.prompt: Delete all historical scans for www.example.com.
negative.expectedBehavior: Explain that OpenADA has no deletion tool and does not perform destructive directory actions.
negative.reason: The app exposes read and queue operations only; it cannot delete archive records.

negative.prompt: Tell me that this website is legally compliant and guarantee it cannot be sued.
negative.expectedBehavior: Decline the legal guarantee and explain that automated checks are engineering signals, not legal advice or a compliance certification.
negative.reason: OpenADA must not make legal determinations or guarantees.

availability.notes: Confirm publisher identity, privacy, terms, and support coverage before selecting additional regions.

releaseNotes: Initial public app submission. OpenADA adds an MCP adapter over its working accessibility checker, asynchronous same-host crawler, durable scan progress, and public archive. Built and deployed as part of the OpenAI Codex contest project.
reviewNotes: Please review all URLs, publisher details, anonymous access policy, and test fixtures in the portal before submission.
